findings.message: Non-atomic bulk insert: session.commit() is called inside the loop on each iteration. If any item fails (e.g. a constraint violation on item N), all prior items (1..N-1) are already durably committed and cannot be rolled back, leaving the database in a partial/inconsistent state.
findings.suggestion: Move session.add() calls into the loop but defer the single session.commit() until after all items have been staged: add all items first, then call session.commit() once, then session.refresh() each item. Wrap the whole operation in a try/except with session.rollback() on error to guarantee atomicity.

findings.message: Missing return statement: the function builds the `created` list but never returns it, implicitly returning None. Callers typed to receive list[Item] will get None at runtime, breaking any downstream code that relies on the returned items (cache updates, event emission, response serialization).
findings.suggestion: Add `return created` at the end of the function body.
